cds.title: Bagaichama najau timi
cds.note: The lyrics of this song FR!! This song will always be in my heart.

cds.title: Dont matter
cds.note: Akery is such a vibe creator. love this song!

cds.title: Life Goes On
cds.note: PAC!!! Honestly my most favourite rapper of all time. This song is my life motto FR!

cds.title: Not Afraid
cds.note: Eminem at his beast. truly an inspiration.

cds.title: Till I Collapse
cds.note: The ultimate pump-up song. The energy is unmatched.

cds.title: Song Cry
cds.note: Jay-Z at his most vulnerable. A timeless classic.

cds.title: PRIDE.
cds.note: Kendrick is a poet. This track is so deep and makes you think twice about everything.

cds.title: Dive
cds.note: The acoustic guitar and Ed's vocals here are just pure magic.

albums.title: Khoj
albums.note: Favorite Song: Khoj. This album is a milestone in Nepali rock history. 1974 AD at their absolute best.

albums.title: My Beautiful Dark Twisted Fantasy
albums.note: Favorite Song: Devil In A New Dress. The production on this album is insane. Every track feels like an anthem.

albums.title: DAMN.
albums.note: Favorite Song: PRIDE. Kendrick is a poet. This album is so deep and makes you think twice about everything.

albums.title: To Pimp A Butterfly
albums.note: Favorite Song: Alright. A truly iconic album that addresses important social issues gracefully.

albums.title: Me Against The World
albums.note: Favorite Song: Me Against The World. This is the album that introduced me to poetry through wordplays. Can feel the authentic words of Tupac on this album FR!

albums.title: Good Kid, M.A.A.D City
albums.favSong: Sing About Me, I'm Dying of Thirst
albums.note: Favorite Song: Sing About Me, I'm Dying of Thirst. poetry in another level.

albums.title: Illmatic
albums.note: Favorite Song: N.Y. State of Mind. Greatest hip hop album of all time. Sleep is the cousin of death.

albums.title: Get Rich Or Die Tryin'
albums.note: The energy on this debut is unmatched.

albums.title: Life After Death
albums.note: Pure East Coast Classic. The king of East, Notorious B.I.G reflected a ruthless persona on this one. Buttersmooth rhymes on a shaky boom box beats. Smells like NYC. effortless flows and when the beats get cold with razorsharp rhymes, Notorious B.I.G proves why he is the king of the East.

albums.title: 2014 Forest Hills Drive
albums.note: Storytelling at its finest. It's like a musical biography.

albums.title: Music To Be Murdered By
albums.note: I remember the first time I heard this album. I was there when it was released.
A masterpiece of production and one of the most influential West Coast rap albums.

albums.title: Born Sinner
albums.note: Cole's growth as an artist is so evident here. Power Trip is a classic.

albums.title: Divide
albums.note: So many hits on this one. Perfect for any mood.

albums.title: Lokgeet Haru
albums.note: Classic Nepali folk music that brings back so many memories.

albums.title: Know-It-All
albums.note: A great debut. Her voice is so unique and soulful.

albums.title: No. 6 Collaborations Project
albums.note: Love the variety of artists on this project. Really shows Ed's versatility.

albums.title: The Off-Season
albums.favSong: m y . l i f e
albums.note: Pure lyricism. Cole is at the top of his game here.

albums.title: The Pains of Growing
albums.note: A beautiful, introspective look at growing up.

albums.title: The Black Album
albums.note: Classic retirement (not really) album. Some of Jay's best work.

albums.title: The Eminem Show
albums.note: Eminem at his peak commercial and critical success.

albums.title: The Marshall Mathers LP
albums.note: One of the fastest-selling albums in history. Raw and unfiltered.

albums.title: X
albums.note: The album that made Ed a global superstar. Every song is a hit.

albums.title: Ellie Greenwich
albums.cover: ../assets/images/music/“Ellie Greenwich Composes, Produces And Sings” (1968, United Artists)  Her first LP.jpg
albums.note: A pioneer in the music industry. Her songwriting and production are legendary.
An incredibly honest and vulnerable album. A late-career masterpiece.

albums.title: Reasonable Doubt
albums.note: The classic debut. Pure street poetry and flawless flow.

albums.title: AM
albums.note: Modern classic. Slick, dark, and incredibly cool.

albums.title: Recovery
albums.note: Life can get pretty hard sometimes, Its unpredictable, But when that time comes when i get badly down. The Recovery album catches me and grabs me tight. Containing songs from thought provoking to soul burning. Its an absollutly amazing album. Eminem created a masterpiece while recovering from drug addiction to take responsibility as a father(Goated momentum). All of the 17 tracks of the album is like a roaring engine. Whereas that beautifully currated wordplays act as fuel to that engine. That drumm.....I'm gonna stop. Go check up that album ASAP! It's beautiful!!

albums.title: The Blueprint
albums.note: Luxury rap at its finest. Flawless production. One of the best Jay-Z albums.

albums.title: Alfredo
albums.note: A modern masterpiece. Freddie is one of the best lyricists out right now.
